texto: You pushed me down to the depths of my sorrow until I thought I wouldn’t see the light of day again. You expected me to drown, didn’t you? When I pull myself from the darkness, I’ll be stronger for it and you’ll regret leaving me for dead.

texto: No matter what, the day didn't feel like Christmas to her.She remembered years ago, when she had been just a little kid, and the word had been enough to make her happy. texto: I want a fairytale romance in a make-believe land. Let’s run through the beautiful meadow and pretend the walls aren’t closing in. One trip and the world will consume us but don’t hesitate and don’t look back. This world of make-believe survives solely on your faith.

texto: In her crazy, upside down world, she needed someone like that. A rock that would keep her going, an anchor to keep her from drifting away. Instead, she was alone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kayla Krantz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “In her crazy, upside down world, she needed someone like that. A rock that would keep her going, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, depression, give-up pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de give-up e loneliness.
